title: Research Temporal error handling best practices

log.context: Research Temporal error handling best practices

Done. Here's a summary of what was researched and applied:

## Research findings (from Temporal Python SDK docs)

- `ApplicationError(msg, non_retryable=True)` is the idiomatic way to signal permanent failures that should not be retried — missing credentials, invalid input, business logic violations
- Bare Python exceptions propagate as retryable failures by default (Temporal wraps them in `ApplicationError`)  
- Workflows should catch `ActivityError` (not bare `Exception`) when handling activit...(truncated)
